cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A conserved-or-monotonic state quantity for heat transformations that tracks unavailable work and distinguishes reversible from irreversible processes.
cases.historical_solution: Entropy as a state function whose change organizes reversible heat transfer and the directionality of thermodynamic processes.
cases.insufficiency: Heat-engine limits, irreversibility, and temperature scales could not be compressed by a simple substance-like heat representation.
cases.pre_world: Heat was analyzed through engines, temperature, caloric remnants, and reversible cycles. Carnot had a performance limit, but no state quantity explained irreversible direction.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: An abstract hereditary unit separated from observable trait expression, allowing inheritance to be tracked independently from phenotype.
cases.historical_solution: Gene as a unit of heredity, paired with genotype/phenotype distinctions.
cases.insufficiency: Breeding regularities required a stable transmissible unit distinct from appearance, development, and environment.
cases.pre_world: Heredity was described through traits, breeding ratios, germ plasm, and Mendelian factors, but visible traits and inherited determinants were conflated.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A spatially distributed physical state assigning influence to every point, so the region between bodies becomes part of the explanation.
cases.historical_solution: Field as a real or mathematically represented spatial quantity mediating electromagnetic phenomena.
cases.insufficiency: Induction, propagation, and spatial patterns between sources made source-only representation awkward.
cases.pre_world: Electricity and magnetism were often represented through sources, forces, action at a distance, fluids, and mechanical analogies.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A statistical measure of uncertainty reduction in a message source, tied to channel limits and coding efficiency.
cases.historical_solution: Information entropy, channel capacity, redundancy, and coding theorems.
cases.insufficiency: Communication systems needed a meaning-independent quantity governing compression, redundancy, noise, and capacity.
cases.pre_world: Telegraph and telephone engineering had bandwidth, signals, noise, and codes, but lacked a general measure independent of message meaning.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A population-level filter that accumulates heritable variations because some variants reproduce more successfully in local conditions.
cases.historical_solution: Natural selection as differential survival and reproduction of heritable variation.
cases.insufficiency: Adapted complexity and branching diversity required a mechanism linking small heritable variation to large-scale change.
cases.pre_world: Species resemblance, adaptation, domestication, fossils, and biogeography were known, but species fixity and separate creation left patterns disconnected.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A unified geometric manifold where space and time coordinates transform together and invariant intervals replace separate absolutes.
cases.historical_solution: Minkowski spacetime as a four-dimensional geometric structure for relativity.
cases.insufficiency: Relativity made separate absolute space and absolute time a poor coordinate system for invariant physical laws.
cases.pre_world: Electrodynamics and mechanics had separate space and time variables. Lorentz transformations worked mathematically, but their geometry was not yet the primitive representation.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A formal machine-like procedure with discrete states and steps that defines effective calculability and its limits.
cases.historical_solution: Turing-machine computability and related formalizations of algorithmic procedure.
cases.insufficiency: Hilbert-style decision questions required a representation of what any mechanical method could do.
cases.pre_world: Mathematicians used procedures, calculability, proof search, and decision problems, but effective procedure was not sharply delimited.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A normalized measure over possible outcomes that supports fair expectation and systematic calculation under uncertainty.
cases.historical_solution: Probability and expectation as mathematical tools for chance and uncertain judgment.
cases.insufficiency: Fair division and risk pricing required a quantitative representation of uncertain possibilities.
cases.pre_world: Uncertainty existed in gambling, law, insurance, and astronomy, but there was no general calculus of chance or fair expectation.

cases.generated_candidate_before_reveal: A conserved transformable quantity that appears in multiple forms and permits cross-domain accounting.
cases.historical_solution: Energy as a conserved quantity unifying mechanical work, heat, and other physical processes.
cases.insufficiency: Conversions among heat, work, electricity, and motion demanded a common accounting variable.
cases.pre_world: Mechanics had work, vis viva, heat, chemical affinity, electricity, and motion, but no accepted conserved transformable quantity spanning them.

counterexamples.name: Discovery of Neptune
counterexamples.result: Concept genesis is not required for every major discovery; some discoveries exploit existing concepts with high precision.
counterexamples.why_it_matters: It improved astronomy through prediction from existing Newtonian mechanics rather than by inventing a new representation.

counterexamples.name: Higgs boson detection
counterexamples.result: Large epistemic gain can occur without a new primitive at discovery time.
counterexamples.type: fact confirmation rather than concept birth
counterexamples.why_it_matters: The experimental discovery confirmed a theoretical entity already embedded in an existing conceptual framework.

counterexamples.name: Phlogiston
counterexamples.result: A concept can be useful locally yet lose long-term value if it fails cross-domain transfer and predictive correction.
counterexamples.why_it_matters: It compressed some combustion language temporarily but blocked better oxygen and conservation-based representations.

counterexamples.name: Vital force
counterexamples.result: Naming a gap is not concept birth unless the representation increases compression and future discovery.
counterexamples.why_it_matters: It named biological difficulty without producing enough durable prediction or mechanistic compression.

predictions.expected_observation: Concept births should cluster where representation mismatch, unexplained regularity, and complexity pressure co-occur.
predictions.falsification_test: Score pre-discovery periods across fields blind to the later concept; compare major concept births with ordinary discoveries.
predictions.text: Major concept births will be preceded by at least three independent pressure families, not just one anomaly.

predictions.expected_observation: High-value generated primitives should match historical concepts by transition type, not by name.
predictions.falsification_test: Give researchers only pre-discovery evidence and pressure labels; ask them to propose a primitive before revealing history.
predictions.text: Generated pre-discovery concepts will resemble historical concepts structurally when the dominant pressure is correctly identified.

predictions.expected_observation: Durable concepts should gain transfer domains; weak concepts should require protective exceptions.
predictions.falsification_test: Compare early phlogiston/vital-force-like concepts against entropy/gene/field-like concepts over later domains.
predictions.text: Invented concepts with low long-term value will show weak applicability expansion after initial compression.

predictions.expected_observation: The protocol should increase structurally new primitives per unit attention while reducing mere synonym invention.
predictions.falsification_test: Run matched AGI or human research labs on unsolved domains with and without this concept-genesis protocol.
predictions.text: Concept birth can be accelerated by forcing pre-concept reconstruction, pressure classification, invariant search, and candidate primitive generation before hypothesis generation.

sources.note: Thermodynamics developed through Carnot, Kelvin, Clausius, heat engines, and the second law.
sources.title: A History of Thermodynamics: The Missing Manual

sources.note: Carnot's heat-engine limit preceded Clausius's rigorous entropy-based formulation.
sources.title: Second law of thermodynamics overview

sources.note: Johannsen coined gene and distinguished genotype from phenotype.
sources.title: 1909: The Word Gene Coined

sources.note: Johannsen's genotype/phenotype distinction reframed hereditary factors.
sources.title: The holist tradition in twentieth century genetics

sources.note: Faraday and Maxwell are treated as modern initiators of the field concept.
sources.title: The field concepts of Faraday and Maxwell

sources.note: Maxwell used Faraday's conceptualization to unify electricity, magnetism, and waves.
sources.title: History of classical field theory

sources.note: Information was formalized statistically through entropy, channel capacity, and coding.
sources.title: Shannon, A Mathematical Theory of Communication

sources.note: Pre-Shannon telegraphy and communication engineering lacked the general statistical measure.
sources.title: Information theory overview

sources.note: Natural selection compressed diverse adaptation, variation, and biogeography into one mechanism.
sources.title: Darwin Online: On the Origin of Species

sources.note: Natural selection depends on variation, differential reproduction, and inheritance.
sources.title: Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy: Natural Selection

sources.note: Minkowski's spacetime reframed Lorentz transformations geometrically.
sources.title: The Historical Origins of Spacetime

sources.note: Minkowski introduced a geometric reading of relativity after Einstein's 1905 work.
sources.title: Hermann Minkowski's Spacetime

sources.note: The algorithm concept moved from calculation procedures to formal computability.
sources.title: Algorithms: From Al-Khwarizmi to Turing and Beyond

sources.note: Turing gave a formal model of effective procedure and computability.
sources.title: Turing, On Computable Numbers

sources.note: Gambling, insurance, and fair division problems preceded formal probability.
sources.title: History of probability

sources.note: Pascal and Fermat's problem of points helped create mathematical expectation.
sources.title: Pascal and the Invention of Probability Theory

sources.note: Work on vis viva, heat, mechanics, and conservation preceded the unified energy concept.
sources.title: Conservation of Energy

sources.note: Joule, Mayer, Helmholtz, and others linked heat and mechanical work.
sources.title: Conservation of Energy: Missing Features in Its Nature and Justification
